other_observers: David and Doris Kline; Doris Kline spotted the birds and I made the positive ID. We jointly decided the flock was approximately 35 birds.
specific_location: 3.5-4 mi west of US 40 on CR 55
description: We quickly put a scope on them and the found they had the red cap, streaked flanks, slightly notched tails.  As I was with another couple who'd  never seen them before, we pulled out the field guide to confirm the identification.   I had seen them in Alaska before so knew what they were as  soon as I saw them in the scope. Behaviors: During the Dec 17, 2011 Christmas bird count in the Granby area, my little group found a flock of about 35 Common Redpolls on Grand Country Rd 55 about 3.5-4 miles west of US 40 (south of Granby).   They were foraging in the alders that were in the gully. They seemed to ignore the willows in the same area, favoring the alders.  Undulating flightCall: twitteringPlumage: Basic
